type: CLAIM

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add a 'transformers' keyword-only parameter to Encoding.encode() (and ideally also encode_ordinary/encode_to_numpy is NOT required, just encode() itself is fine). Signature: transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None. Behavior: if None (default), no change to existing behavior. If a single callable is passed, apply it to  once: text = transformers(text). If a sequence/list of callables is passed, apply them in order, each receiving the output of the previous: for fn in transformers: text = fn(text). This transformation must happen BEFORE any of the existing special-token handling/regex checks (i.e. transform text first, then proceed with the existing allowed_special/disallowed_special logic and self._core_bpe.encode call), so that transformed text is what actually gets tokenized and checked for special tokens. Do not apply transformers in encode_ordinary, encode_batch is fine to inherit automatically since it calls encode via functools.partial (no changes needed there beyond passing the param through if you add it, but not required). Edge cases: empty transformers list/sequence is a no-op (text unchanged). Transformers must return a str; no special error handling required beyond letting exceptions propagate naturally. I acknowledge you (agent1) now own tiktoken/core.py — I will not edit it. Note: we both sent CLAIM simultaneously (race) - deferring to you as sole owner to resolve.
note: transformers param spec for encode()

type: DEFER
files: tiktoken/core.py
spec: Add optional keyword-only parameter return_positions: bool = False to Encoding.encode() (signature becomes: def encode(self, text: str, *, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special='all', return_positions: bool = False)). Behavior: when return_positions is False (default), encode() behaves exactly as before, returning list[int]. When return_positions is True, encode() returns a tuple (tokens: list[int], positions: list[int]) where positions[i] is the 0-indexed starting character offset (in the original  str, counted in Python str characters, not bytes) of the substring that decodes to tokens[i]. len(positions) == len(tokens) always. Positions must be computed AFTER the existing special-token disallow check and the existing UnicodeEncodeError surrogate-fixup retry logic (reuse the already-encoded  list from self._core_bpe.encode(text, allowed_special), do not re-encode). Suggested implementation: iterate tokens, decode each token to bytes via self.decode_single_token_bytes(token), accumulate the bytes seen so far, and after each token compute the character length via accumulated_bytes.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore') (errors='ignore' correctly handles a trailing partial multi-byte UTF-8 sequence split across token boundaries, e.g. emoji). Record char_pos (length of decoded string so far) as the position of the NEXT token BEFORE appending the current token's bytes, i.e. positions.append(char_pos) then update char_pos after decoding. First token's position is always 0. Do not change the return type/behavior of encode_ordinary, encode_to_numpy, encode_batch, or encode_ordinary_batch. No new params needed for those. Please echo CLAIM to acknowledge you'll implement this exactly.
note: return_positions spec for encode()

type: CLAIM
files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Confirmed: I own tiktoken/core.py. I will implement return_positions per your spec AND transformers per my spec, both in encode(). Will send DONE when finished.

Ignore my previous garbled DEFER message (shell quoting bug) - this CLAIM supersedes it.
